_title: Magic Judge — your Magic: The Gathering judge inside your AI
_desc: The Magic: The Gathering judge inside your AI assistant — rules questions answered with official citations, live card search and bracket-aware deck building. Free and open source.
badge: Free & open source · From players, to players
p2p: Unofficial fan project — made by a player, for players.
tagline: The Magic: The Gathering judge that lives inside your AI assistant.
sub: Rules questions answered with the official rule cited, step-by-step verdicts, live card search and bracket-aware deck building — in Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor or any app that speaks MCP.
feat_sub: Five abilities, one for each color of mana.
f1p: Answers cite the rule number straight from the Comprehensive Rules — an always up-to-date local source, never "from memory".
f2p: Complex card interactions resolved step by step: stack order, priority and the final game state.
f3p: Names in English or Portuguese, format legality, prices and official rulings — always current.
f4p: Mana curve, community-recommended synergies, Commander brackets (1–5) and up-to-date Game Changers.
f5p: A beginner-friendly knowledge base — from a quick-start guide to the official basic rulebook.
demo_sub: Real question, real answer — with the rules cited.
demo_q: I blocked a Colossal Dreadmaw (6/6, trample) with a Typhoid Rats (1/1, deathtouch). What happens?
demo_a:
Verdict: the opponent takes 5 damage and both creatures die.

The Dreadmaw only needs to assign 1 point of lethal damage to its blocker (rule 702.19, Trample) — the other 5 trample through. The Rats destroys the Dreadmaw with 1 point of deathtouch damage (rules 702.2b and 704.5h).
how_sub: Magic Judge speaks MCP — the open standard that plugs new abilities into AI assistants. Pick your app, paste one link, done.
s1p:
Open Claude → Settings → Connectors. Tap + Add custom connector, paste the link below and hit Connect.
Tip: set the tools to “Always allow” so Claude won't ask every time.
s2note: The same link works in Cursor, VS Code and any other MCP client.
s3p1: Skills add the method on top of the connector — how to look up rules, search cards and build decks by bracket.
s3p2: Download the pack, then in Claude go to Settings → Skills and add them.
ask1: "I blocked a trampler with a deathtouch blocker — what happens?"
ask2: "How much is a Sol Ring? Is it legal in Modern?"
ask3: "Build a Krenko Commander deck, bracket 2, under $50"
sup_sub: Donations keep the server running and the rules database updated with every new set.
pix_p: Every bit helps keep the judge on call.
intl_p: Prefer to support from abroad? (Ko-fi, PayPal...)
road_sub: Requests, suggestions and improvements are welcome.
contact: Requests, suggestions and improvements: open an issue on GitHub
